# kemp Provider

Provider for Progress Kemp LoadMaster.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    kemp = {
      source  = "pier62350/kemp"
      version = "~> 0.1"
    }
  }
}

# All credentials are typically read from environment variables:
#   KEMP_HOST, KEMP_API_KEY (or KEMP_USERNAME/KEMP_PASSWORD)
provider "kemp" {
  host    = "https://10.0.0.5:9443"
  api_key = var.kemp_api_key
}

variable "kemp_api_key" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) API key. May also be set via the `KEMP_API_KEY` environment variable. Mutually exclusive with `username`/`password` (api_key wins if both are set).
- `host` (String) Base URL of the LoadMaster, including scheme and port (e.g. `https://10.0.0.5:9443`). May also be set via the `KEMP_HOST` environment variable.
- `insecure_skip_verify` (Boolean) Skip TLS certificate verification. Defaults to true since LoadMasters typically present self-signed certificates. Set to false to enforce verification.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password for basic auth. May also be set via the `KEMP_PASSWORD` environment variable.
- `username` (String) Username for basic auth. May also be set via the `KEMP_USERNAME` environment variable.
